citation_short: TIME, 'Beirut: A Fortress Under Heavy Fire' (19 Jul 1982)
title: Beirut: A Fortress Under Heavy Fire
date_scope: 1982-07-19 with references to the siege from June onward
strengths: Contemporaneous, explicitly identifies the encircled civilian population and the approximate number of PLO fighters slated for evacuation.
limitations: Journalistic estimate rather than a census; fighter count is approximate and framed in the context of negotiation.
notes: Key denominator anchor for the West Beirut siege.

citation_short: Adelman, 'Miscounting Refugees: a Case Study of Lebanon' (Refuge, Sep 1982)
title: Miscounting Refugees: a Case Study of Lebanon
geographic_scope: Southern Lebanon; West Beirut; Lebanon-wide refugee/displacement categories
strengths: Precisely disaggregates refugee, homeless, and temporarily displaced categories and provides explicit south-Lebanon camp and refugee denominators.
limitations: Focuses on displacement and homelessness rather than full exposed civilian population for all southern districts; some figures are explicitly approximate.
notes: Best denominator source for south-Lebanon refugee populations and West Beirut displacement.

citation_short: Sayigh, 'Palestinian Military Performance in the 1982 War' (JPS, Summer 1983)
title: Palestinian Military Performance in the 1982 War
geographic_scope: Lebanon, with deployment detail for the south and Beirut
strengths: Conflict-specific scholarly reconstruction of Palestinian deployment, distinguishing regulars from part-time/local support and separating south from Beirut.
limitations: Focuses on Palestinian forces and excludes some Syrian/PLA personnel from the headline Beirut and south numbers.
notes: Crucial denominator source for military person-months.

title: The War of Numbers
geographic_scope: Lebanon, with specific references to Beirut and south-Lebanon camps
strengths: Excellent source for explaining why casualty counts diverged; preserves contemporaneous low and high narratives and highlights Palestinian-civilian undercounting.
limitations: Not a systematic registry; mixes official, NGO, and eyewitness claims of varying evidentiary quality.
notes: Best source for documenting disagreement rather than for a single clean episode estimate.

citation_short: Washington Post on An Nahar casualty survey (3 Sep 1982)
title: War Casualties Put at 48000 in Lebanon
author_or_org: The Washington Post reporting An Nahar data
geographic_scope: Lebanon; Beirut and southern Lebanon broken out
strengths: Provides a geographically split An Nahar hospital/police-based tally with separate south civilian and military counts.
limitations: The full article was not accessible in-session; Beirut breakdown is mixed-status only and reported by snippet.
notes: Useful for bounding south casualties and total Beirut deaths.

citation_short: Christian Science Monitor on Lebanese police figures (21 Dec 1982)
title: New figures emerge on the cost in lives of Israel's war in Lebanon
strengths: Gives a concise police-based Beirut total and civilian share that can be translated into a status split for the siege episode.
limitations: Only the search snippet was accessible in-session; exact underlying police tables were not retrieved.
notes: Core input for the derived civilian/military split in West Beirut.

citation_short: Mandel, 'Israel in 1982: The War in Lebanon' (AJYB 1984)
title: Israel in 1982: The War in Lebanon
geographic_scope: Lebanon, with Beirut and southern districts
strengths: Captures official Israeli-era reporting, including evacuation totals and low official civilian counts for Tyre, Sidon, and Nabatiyeh.
limitations: Strongly reflects Israeli official reporting and therefore functions better as a low-official bound than as a neutral casualty baseline.
notes: Useful for sensitivity bounds and for the evacuation episode.

citation_short: Final Report of the Commission of Inquiry into the Events at the Refugee Camps in Beirut (1983)
title: Final Report of the Commission of Inquiry into the Events at the Refugee Camps in Beirut
geographic_scope: Sabra and Shatila camps, West Beirut
strengths: Primary legal inquiry establishing that Phalangist forces entered the camps and that civilian risk was foreseen.
limitations: Not a casualty census and does not solve denominator or combatant-exposure questions for DR estimation.
notes: Primary source for inflicting-side attribution in the Sabra-Shatila episode.

citation_short: EBSCO Research Starter, 'Palestinians Are Massacred in West Beirut'
title: Palestinians Are Massacred in West Beirut
strengths: Aggregates widely cited basic facts on the siege and Sabra-Shatila in a single accessible summary.
limitations: Tertiary rather than primary; should not override contemporaneous police, legal, or scholarly sources.
notes: Used only for auxiliary Sabra-Shatila population/death context.

title: Lebanon War Casualties
author_or_org: Interactive Encyclopedia of the Palestine Question / Institute for Palestine Studies
date_scope: 1982-06-04 to 1982-08-31, plus Israeli military losses to 1982-10-11
strengths: Accessible structured summary of the Lebanese police conservative total and civilian share.
limitations: Not episode-specific enough for direct DR estimation and does not solve the Beirut-specific status split on its own.
notes: Best used as family-level context and as a check on aggregate plausibility.
